channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Neurotechnology could revolutionize work—but it comes with big risks for workers
item.description: Despite all the attention on technologies that reduce the hands-on role of humans at work – such as self-driving vehicles, robot workers, artificial intelligence and so on – researchers in the field of neuroergonomics are using technology to improve how humans perform in their roles at work.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Los CDO pasan a ser el centro de atención de las estrategias empresariales
item.description: Dado que los datos son fundamentales para todos los aspectos de la empresa, el director de Datos se ha convertido en un ejecutivo altamente estratégico. El CDO de hoy se centra en ayudar a la organización a aprovechar los datos como un activo empresarial para impulsar los resultados.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Rising costs and workplace challenges are worrying procurement workers everywhere
item.description: Despite the promise of higher budgets, businesses are being met with increasing costs as they struggle with procurement.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: AMD and Nvidia could be set for epic GPU showdown with RX 9070 and 9070 XT going on sale at the same time as the RTX 5080
item.description: AMD RX 9070 and 9070 XT graphics cards might be here sooner than you think – pre-orders could go live on January 23.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Los CIO están replanteándose cómo utilizan los servicios de nube pública
item.description: En los últimos años, las empresas se han esforzado por trasladar la mayor cantidad posible de recursos lo más rápido posible a la nube pública para minimizar los gastos de capital y ahorrar dinero. Sin embargo, cada vez más, los CIO están revisando y racionalizando dichas inversiones.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Worst leadership advice you’ve ever received
item.description: At some point in your career, you’ve been given bad advice. It’s usually well meaning. But maybe it is dated or exposes a problematic world view in the advice-giver that you’d be better off not emulating. Even bad advice can be a good teacher, though.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Estas son las prioridades de inversión en ‘tech’ para 2025
item.description: La inversión en TI está experimentando un interesante incremento. Las previsiones de la firma de análisis Gartner apuntan a un crecimiento en Europa del dinero destinado por las organizaciones a esta partida del 8,7% en 2025.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Año nuevo, nueva apuesta por la innovación digital en Heineken
item.description: En 2019, Heineken comenzó un verdadero viaje de transformación, cuando Ralph Ostertag, director de Tecnología y Digitalización para Asia Pacífico , se incorporó a la empresa tras haber trabajado en Mercedes Benz y, años antes, en otras industrias.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google faces privacy lawsuit as judge highlights data governance concerns
item.description: A federal judge in San Francisco has ruled that a privacy lawsuit against Google, alleging the company improperly collected personal data from mobile devices, can proceed.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta tests eBay listings in Facebook Marketplace to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Meta rolls back hate speech rules as Zuckerberg cites 'recent elections' as a catalyst
item.description: It wasn’t just fact-checking that Meta scrapped from its platforms as it prepares for the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jeff Bezos and Lauren Sanchez to marry in Italy: Source
item.description: Amazon billionaire and his blushing bride-to-be, Lauren Sanchez, held a lovely dinner party over the holidays at Matsuhisa in Aspen.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Cisco in 2025: Lots of hard work ahead
item.description: For a company of its size, Cisco executed a fairly short list of technology rollouts in 2024 and made a surprising lack of major updates to its core networking portfolio, which might have contributed to the 23% decline in networking revenue Cisco reported in its latest quarterly financials.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OnePlus' rumored 'mini' flagship could be the final nail in the coffin for small Android phones
item.description: The rumored Oppo Find X8 Mini could be coming to global markets via OnePlus, the company’s West-focused brand – but what does a 6.31-inch mini phone mean for the future of small handsets?
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: ByteDance's Lemon8 gains traction amid TikTok ban threat as creators push the app
item.description: Hearing a lot about Lemon8 lately? You’re not the only one. Amid a looming U.S. ban on TikTok, content creators have been pushing the platform's sister app.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tech industry sounds alarm over US AI Export Control Framework
item.description: The Biden administration’s recently introduced “Export Control Framework for Artificial Intelligence Diffusion” has sparked a heated debate in the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: CES 2025: Vay Expands Las Vegas Fleet of Remotely Driven EVs to Over 100 Cars
item.description: Vay, a company that offers remotely driven electric vehicles, is expanding its fleet in Las Vegas to over 100 cars. The expansion aims to meet growing demand and reduce wait times.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Oracle offers price-performance boost with Exadata X11M update
item.description: The new version of Oracle’s Exadata platform, X11M, is now generally available, and promises improved performance on generative AI tasks such as retrieval augmented generation (RAG) for the same price as the old version.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Egypt unveils ancient rock-cut tombs and burial shafts in Luxor
item.description: Egypt has unveiled several discoveries near the famed city of Luxor including ancient rock-cut tombs and burial shafts dating back 3,600 years.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Building generative AI applications is too hard, developers say
item.description: When everyone touts the benefits of generative artificial intelligence, telling the world how much easier it will make all of our lives, many developers who actually have to build those miracle apps are of a different opinion, according to a new survey sponsored by IBM.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Japan links Chinese hacker MirrorFace to dozens of cyberattacks targeting security and tech data
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan on Wednesday linked more than 200 cyberattacks over the past five years targeting the country's national security and high technology data to a Chinese hacking group, MirrorFace, detailing their tactics and calling on government agencies and businesses to reinforce preventive meas
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meta eliminates fact-checking in latest bow to Trump
item.description: Meta chief executive officer Mark Zuckerberg on Tuesday announced changes to content moderation on Facebook and Instagram long sought by conservatives. Incoming President Donald Trump said the new approach was “probably” due to threats he made against the technology mogul.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, BARBARA ORTUTAY and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man used ChatGPT to plan Las Vegas Cybertruck blast
item.description: The man killed in the Las Vegas Cybertruck blast used ChatGPT to plan the explosion, police said. In a press conference, Tuesday, Las Vegas police released more details of the intentions of 37-year-old Matthew Livelsberger, who died of a gunshot wound prior to the car exploding.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: OpenAI is losing money on its pricey ChatGPT Pro subscription
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, in a post on X, says the AI ​​company is currently losing money on its ChatGPT Pro subscription. “People are using it much more than we expected,” he wrote. The company introduced its ChatGPT Pro subscription in December.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Facebook to end fact-checking, boost political content
item.description: The company said automated moderation would focus on serious issues like terrorism and child sexual exploitation instead.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Why Apple’s AI-driven reality distortion matters
item.description: Apple has been forced to admit what every company involved in artificial intelligence (AI) should also be forced to state — AI makes mistakes, just like people do. On the surface, it’s not a terribly big deal: Apple’s AI badly mangled a handful of news headlines.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How adding capacity to a network could reduce IT costs
item.description: Finding an enterprise looking to spend more on network equipment is like finding gold while digging for worms for bait. Cost management used to be an option in enhancing profits, but these days it’s a mantra.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta's new board includes UFC boss Dana White, a familiar figure in Trump's orbit
item.description: Meta has appointed three new members to its board of directors, including Dana White, the president and CEO of Ultimate Fighting Championship and a familiar figure in the orbit of incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Lost your contacts on your phone? Here's how to get them back
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson provides two methods to restore your contacts for iPhone and Android devices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nvidia's new GPU series led an avalanche of entertainment-related announcements at CES
item.description: Nvidia unveiled its GeForce RTX 50 Series desktop and laptop GPUs — powered by its new Blackwell artificial intelligence chip — kicking off a string of entertainment-related AI announcements and discussions at the trade show, including several terse conversations about regulation of the budding tech
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Microsoft will invest $80B in AI data centers in fiscal 2025
item.description: Microsoft is on track to invest around $80 billion this fiscal year to build out AI-enabled data centers, company president Brad Smith said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg denies claims of building doomsday bunker in Hawaii
item.description: "I think it got blown out of proportion as if the whole ranch was some kind of doomsday bunker," Zuckerberg said.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: OPM CIO Cavallo retiring after 23 years in government
item.description: Guy Cavallo has been the Office of Personnel Management’s CIO since 2020 and also worked at SBA, TSA and began his career working for the Senate. The post OPM CIO Cavallo retiring after 23 years in government first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cyber security agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AI PCs
item.description: Virtually every aspect of technology has been affected, and potentially improved, by artificial intelligence (AI). PCs are no exception. While the meaning of “AI PC” is still evolving, it generally refers to a computer specifically designed to excel at AI-powered tasks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: China rolls out its crime-fighting ball to chase down criminals
item.description: A Chinese company has developed a spherical, 276-pound law enforcement robot that has been chasing down criminals at impressive speeds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top network and data center events 2025
item.description: Ready to travel to gain hands-on experience with new networking and infrastructure tools? Tech conferences – in person and virtual – give attendees a chance to access product demos, network with peers, earn continuing education credits, and catch a celebrity keynote or live entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Network World
